sections.content:
After moving LEFT:
1. Player moved from [28,33]-[31,35] to [28,29]-[31,31]
2. The top c frame at [24,28]-[27,31] is still c (didn't convert to G)
3. The b frame and gray bar are back at the right
So moving LEFT didn't convert the top c frame to G. The player is at [28,29]-[31,31], and the top c frame is at [24,28]-[27,31]. The player is below the c frame, adjacent in rows (28 and 27), and overlapping in columns (29-31 and 28-31).
The c frame didn't convert to G because the player didn't move closer to it. The player was at cols 33-35, and the c frame is at cols 28-31. Moving LEFT brought the player to cols 29-31, which overlaps with the c frame's columns. But the row distance didn't change (player is still at rows 28-31, c frame at rows 24-27).
Hmm, maybe the c frame only converts to G when the player moves horizontally toward it (same row range), not vertically.
Let me think about this differently. The earlier successful conversion was:
- Player at [28,32]-[31,35], c frame at [28,24]-[31,27] (same row range)
- Player moved LEFT to [28,29]-[31,31], c frame converted to G
In this case, the player and c frame were in the same row range (28-31), and the player moved horizontally toward the c frame.
Now, the top c frame is at [24,28]-[27,31], which is in a different row range (24-27) from the player (28-31). So maybe the c frame only converts to G when the player is in the same row range and moves horizontally toward it.
To convert the top c frame to G, I need to move the player to the same row range as the c frame (rows 24-27). But the player is 4 rows tall, so moving the player to rows 24-27 would require moving UP by 4 rows.
Wait, but earlier when I tried moving UP, it had no impact. Let me check if the player can move UP at all.
Actually, looking at the ascii, the player is at rows 28-31, and there's nothing blocking the player from moving UP. The top c frame is at rows 24-27, which is above the player.
Let me try moving UP multiple times to move the player into the same row range as the top c frame.
But wait, earlier when I tried moving UP once, it had no impact. Maybe the player can only move UP when it's not adjacent to the c frame.
Actually, let me re-examine. The player is at [28,29]-[31,31], and the top c frame is at [24,28]-[27,31]. The player's top row is 28, and the c frame's bottom row is 27. They are adjacent. So moving UP would move the player into the c frame, which might be blocked.
But the player is at cols 29-31, and the c frame is at cols 28-31. They overlap in columns. So the player can't move UP because it would collide with the c frame.
To move UP, I need to first move the player to a column range that doesn't overlap with the c frame. The c frame is at cols 28-31. If I move the player to cols 32-35 (by moving RIGHT), then the player doesn't overlap with the c frame in columns, and I can move UP.
Let me try: RIGHT, UP, UP, UP, UP (to move the player above the c frame), then LEFT to approach the c frame.
Wait, but that's a lot of actions. Let me think about whether there's a shorter way.
Actually, maybe I should focus on the left c frame first, since it's in the same row range as the player (rows 28-31). The left c frame is at [28,16]-[31,19], and the player is at [28,29]-[31,31]. Moving LEFT would bring the player closer to the left c frame, potentially converting it to G.
Let me try moving LEFT.
